uplink.description: CO2 frame (0x13, 'new' sensor): two 17-byte measurement records (newest last) + trailing battery%. Each record carries temperature(/100), humidity(/2), pressure(Pascal/100 -> hPa), illuminance(lux), bVOC(3-byte ppb) and CO2(signed ppm). Newest is at the top level; the older record is in history (newest-first). expected.data authored by running the codec; per-measurement values cross-checked against reference/upstream-codec.js parseCo2 (temp 23.5/24.12, hum 45/44, pressure 1013.25/998.5, lux 450/1320, voc 560/610, co2 812/905, battery 91); timestamps emitted as deterministic RFC3339 (upstream uses locale strings) and battery-% as the camelCase extra batteryPercent (vocabulary battery is volts).
uplink._note: Synthetic in-bounds CO2 (0x13) frame; expected.data is the output of this folder's codec.js, with each measurement field independently verified against reference/upstream-codec.js (parseCo2 path).

uplink.description: CO2 frame (0x13) with a single measurement record + trailing battery% (no history). Confirms the newest reading sits at the top level and batteryPercent is captured when only one record is present.
uplink._note: Synthetic single-record 0x13 frame; expected.data is this codec's output, fields cross-checked against reference/upstream-codec.js.

uplink.description: TER frame (0x04): three temperature/humidity/pressure records (no light/VOC/CO2), no trailing battery byte. Mirrors the Enginko data-frame-format TER worked example reused from the mcf-lw12co2 sibling; the CO2E firmware also emits this base frame. Newest record at top level, the two older ones in history (newest-first). Per-record values cross-checked against reference/upstream-codec.js parseTER.
uplink._note: TER (0x04) base frame; bytes match the Enginko data-frame-format example, expected.data is this codec's output (per-record values verified against reference/upstream-codec.js parseTER).

uplink.description: Unsupported frame id (0x09 metering) is rejected.
